mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in FEDFUNDS transmits through lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day SPX forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of months when FEDFUNDS falls over the prior month, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS accelerates sharply above its recent trend, it indicates improving household and business spending that supports near-term corporate revenue surprises and pushes S&P 500 returns higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered observations do not deliver positive 5-day SPX returns more than 50% of the time, or if the average 5-day return after triggers is not above the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE combined with slowing PAYEMS growth signals weakening labor income and demand, which should compress equity earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months and PAYEMS growth is below 0.1% over the same span
failure_condition: Rejected if the combined trigger does not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases, or if UNRATE and PAYEMS effects are individually sufficient without the interaction term.

mechanism_narrative: A broad increase in INDPRO reflects stronger real output and industrial utilization, which improves earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if 60-day forward SPX returns are not above baseline after positive INDPRO trend periods, or if the sign of the relationship is negative in more than two consecutive rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS level above neutral tightens discounting and raises recession risk, so when policy stays restrictive the S&P 500 should underperform over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20-day SPX returns are not negative in at least 55% of months with FEDFUNDS above 4.5%, or if high-rate periods do not show worse returns than low-rate periods by a statistically meaningful margin.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth remains positive while UNRATE falls, labor income and confidence improve together, creating a demand-led lift to earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month and UNRATE falls month-over-month
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint condition does not outperform single-variable PAYEMS or UNRATE signals, or if triggered 5-day SPX returns are not positive more often than not.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST after prior strength indicates cooling housing demand and weaker future consumption, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% from its 3-month high
failure_condition: Rejected if 60-day forward SPX returns are not below baseline after the trigger, or if the signal remains negative even when housing declines are not followed by weaker broader demand measures.

mechanism_narrative: In a recovery phase, simultaneous improvement in GDPC1 and RSAFS signals broad demand reacceleration, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as revenue expectations rise.
failure_condition: Rejected if the joint GDPC1-RSAFS condition does not outperform either series alone, or if 60-day SPX returns are not positive in recovery episodes with concurrent gains in both proxies.
